title: mericarpaea ciliata
intro: Mericarpaea ciliata, also known as the woolly bitter pea, is a flowering plant species in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Mericarpaea ciliata is often used as a tea or infused in oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
